steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence says Bitter's feet were 'singing the news.' The question asks what function this figurative language serves in the passage. The key is what 'singing the news' communicates: Bitter's footsteps are delivering information to Jam.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — [Aloe's reaction]: the metaphor is about Bitter's footsteps telling Jam something — Aloe is not the recipient of this signal. ✗
  • Choice B — [Jam prefers music]: nothing in the passage supports that Jam has a preference for music; this misreads the metaphor literally. ✗
  • Choice C — Bitter's footsteps reveal something to Jam: matches the metaphor exactly — her feet are 'singing the news,' meaning her walk communicates to Jam that the painting is finished. ✓
  • Choice D — [Bitter always sings]: conflates the figurative 'singing' with literal singing. The passage does not say Bitter habitually sings. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
